text:
From: Caroline Nguyen <c.nguyen@smallcard.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 15:24:00 -0800
Subject: Re: SDK question — p99 spikes with small batch sizes

Hi Jonas,

Quick note from our QA team: when we set the SDK batch window to very small values (max_inflight=2 and per_request_batch_max_tokens=64) our p99 for single-turn generation jumps from ~320ms to ~1.8s during peak traffic. We attached the latency trace and the CSV of sampling windows. This started after yesterday's client-side SDK bump (v1.8.3).

Any chance this is a runtime-side behavior? We are in the middle of a pilot and need to understand whether a config change or a platform fix is required before we expand to production.

Thanks,
Caroline
Senior SRE, Smallcard Inc.
Attachment: smallcard_latency_trace.png, smallcard_batch_metrics.csv

text:
From: Ana Ruiz <ana.ruiz@fablehealth.com>
To: dylan.brooks@redwood.ai
Date: Mon, 05 Oct 2026 15:45:00 -0500
Subject: Hosted API — severe tail latency for 32k prompts (tenant:fable)

Hi Dylan,

Today at ~11:30am PT our production integration started seeing huge tail latency on calls that include long patient-history prompts (~28k–32k tokens). p95 climbed from ~220ms to 3.2s and p99 to 8.7s; error rate spiked briefly to 6%. This is hitting our scheduled summarization jobs and causing timeouts in our pipeline.

We tried reducing concurrency and that helped marginally, but the behavior persists and is intermittent. I’ve attached a short sample of request IDs and timestamps below.

Sample request ids: rwd-ff8c-9a2c, rwd-ff9d-3b1e, rwd-ffa1-4c6b
Approx prompt sizes: 27,900 — 32,100 tokens

This is impacting a critical customer workflow and we need an ETA on root cause and mitigation. Can yo...

Date: Mon, 24 Aug 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Redlines: Exhibit D - Pricing Schedule and Fee Lift Mechanism

Chloe —

Attached is Acme's redline to Exhibit D (pricing schedule). The primary open items are:

1) Fee lift mechanism (Section 2(b)): Acme's finance team pushed back on a single, uncapped percentage step when monthly committed usage is exceeded. They're asking for either a gradual step-up (tiered) or an annual smoothing credit so spikes don't create a retrospective lift for one month that skews the whole year.

2) Caps & floors: We propose preserving the floor (minimum monthly fee) but ask for a hard annual cap on aggregate uplift so budget doesn't blow out.

text:
From: Kristen Martinez <kristen@omnidata.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-18T15:03:00-07:00
Subject: Clarify billing for hybrid prompts & bursty traffic

Hi Logan,

Quick question on billing units — we're trying to finalize our internal cost model ahead of the commit call next week and need to understand how Redwood will count tokens and apply minimums for our workload. A few specifics: 

- We have a hybrid pattern: ~65% short API calls (<250 tokens roundtrip), ~35% long-context chains where we pass ~30k-token context (ingestion + lookup) but rely heavily on prefix KV-cache reuse across calls.
- ~12% of requests use streaming outputs (server pushes partial tokens), the rest are regular non-streamed responses.
- We see very bursty traffic: short spikes that hit ~10k RPS for 30–90s during big batch jobs, otherwise steady baseline.

Questions:
1) For long-context r...

To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-19T09:14:00-07:00
Subject: SDK streaming disconnects after long prompt

Hey Ben,

Thanks again for the demo this morning — Samir and I were able to exercise the streaming SDK during a long-document summarization flow. We hit an issue when the combined prompt + context exceeded ~2.5k tokens: the client shows a streaming disconnect and the server returns a 502 briefly. Re-attaching minimal repro logs and a short video clip.

Questions: is there a known limit on streaming/session length? Is there a recommended client-side retry pattern we should use to avoid partial outputs? We're evaluating Redwood for a critical feature so any guidance helps.

Attachments: velvet_demo_logs.zip (200KB), screen-clip.mp4 (referenced)

—Maria
Head of Product, VelvetHost

text:
From: Alex Jensen <alex.jensen@tidalwave.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: SRE Team <sre@tidalwave.ai>
Date: Sun, 9 Aug 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Spike in 5xx + token latency hitting inference — urgent

Hi Logan / Marissa,

We started seeing a sharp jump in 5xx errors and token latency around 08:45 UTC this morning on our production inference endpoints (model: rw-large-v1, region: us-west-2). Symptoms: p50 ~40ms (normal), p95 up to 1.2s, p99 up to 3.6s, and a 5xx rate rising from ~0.2% to 6% over the past 30 minutes. Our app logs show a lot of DNS timeouts followed by backend 502/504 responses.

What we’ve done so far:
- Rolled a small test client to a different VPC subnet — same behavior.
- Captured tcpdump during the window and attached a pcap.
- No config changes on our side since yesterday.

Attached: packet_capture_080...

Subject: Cartology trial: proposed success metrics, eval split, and next steps

Hi Jamie / Priya — thanks again for a productive discovery today. Quick recap plus a concrete proposal so we can lock the scope and calendar for the trial.

TL;DR proposal (please confirm):
- Hybrid evaluation: 1 week offline evaluation on a labeled seed (sanitized) dataset + 3 weeks online shadow against live traffic (shadowing only; no production writes).
- Success criteria (pass/fail gates):

Subject: FinWorks hosted pilot — quickstart checklist, endpoint partitioning & rate plan

Hi Elena — thanks again for the demo today, and for walking us through the FinWorks fraud-review flows. Quick recap and a couple of asks so we can size a hosted pilot that aligns with your latency and throughput needs.

What we heard today (summary):
- Primary use: synchronous risk scoring and bot-detection enrichment called from your fraud pipeline (~80% of calls, 120ms target p50).
- Secondary: async batch re-score for nightly reconciliation (lower SLO).
- Expected pilot traffic: 1k TPS peak during business hours, average 150rps; message size ~300 tokens in/out.
- Data residency: US-only; n...

We're ready to kick off a small pilot (3 internal teams, low monthly spend) but our internal PO process is taking 6+ weeks and we need to get infra time scheduled. Is there an established temporary "no-PO" intake or invoice-only path Redwood can use for short pilots?

What we can provide quickly: statement of work (attached), vendor questionnaire answers, and a PO once procurement completes the formal flow — but that will be after the pilot start. For now we need an invoice route or short-term exception approval.

- Confirm the benchmark test harness you'll run (input traces, sequence lengths, concurrency).
- Share target latency and per-token cost bands for both hosted and dedicated modes.
- Confirm which model variants (quantized vs FP16) you recommend for our dataset.
